title: Assembly Elections 2026 LIVE updates: Will resolve Gorkha issue in constitutional manner without dividing Bengal, says Amit Shah

content: The situation is particularly severe in areas such as Thiruvambady and Koodaranji, where many natural water sources have dried up

content: MSME Minister Kondapalli Srinnivas, MLA Pusapati Aditi Vijayalakshmi and others paid floral tributes to B.R. Ambedkar on his 135th birth anniversary in Vizianagaram

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Mango farmers reiterate demand for pulp-making factory in K.V. Kuppam constituency
content: K.V. Kuppam Assembly constituency’s dependence on agriculture is due to lack of industrial units and factories which can generate jobs for the unemployed educated youth in the region, many of whom are forced migrate to big cities like Chennai, Bengaluru, and Coimbatore in search of employment.

title: T.N. election: J.P. Nadda releases BJP manifesto, says DMK rule has turned State into crime capital
content: Terming the “DMK a Dynasty, Money, and Kickbacks party,” he claimed that DMK president and Chief Minister M.K. Stalin was “not only corrupt, but also a protector of the corrupt”

title: CM condemns Nithin Raj’s death as public sentiment galvanises against management of Anjarakandy dental college
content: CM Pinarayi Vijayan said the Central government has turned a deaf ear to the demand to enact the Rohith Vemula (Prevention of Discrimination, Exclusion or Injustice) (Right to Education and Dignity) Bill

title: Samrat Choudhary set to become new Bihar CM
content: BJP elects Deputy CM Samrat Choudhary as its Legislature Party leader after the resignation of Nitish Kumar

title: Telangana sizzles as temperatures cross 43°C; all 33 districts record above 41°C
content: Aloor in Nizamabad is hottest in Telangana as it records 43.2°C. Maximum temperature in Hyderabad is 41°C.

title: T.N. election: Even as parties promise host of schemes, farmers worried about future of agriculture
content: Despite political promises, younger generations show little interest in agriculture’s future, say several farmers

title: Kurnool police shift two ganja addicts to rehabilitation centre
content: The police decided to bring about a change among the ganja addicted youngsters instead of taking legal action keeping in view their future

title: China says policy to improve relations with India remains unchanged
content: China has been publishing its names for various regions in Arunachal Pradesh since 2017 for which India has been consistently objecting, saying that “fictitious names" to Indian territory cannot alter "undeniable reality"

title: Congress confident of winning all 13 seats in Kozhikode
content: Citing strong UDF support and historical victory margins, the Congress expresses confidence in winning all 13 Kozhikode seats

title: Overspeeding, inattention causing fatal road accidents in Vizianagaram, says SP
content: Vizianagaram SP A.R. Damodar said lack of attention and overspeeding are causing fatal accidents and called for public cooperation to improve road safety

title: Kerala electorate has voted for ‘change of government’, says Shafi Parambil
content: The Vadakara MP says that the ruling CPI(M) will not be able to “compartmentalise” this popular verdict as the “consolidation of votes” by members of a particular caste, religion, or community

title: Noida workers’ protest: Over 300 held, seven FIRs registered; ‘syndicate’ angle under probe, says police
content: “Route marches have been conducted continuously since 5 a.m. This morning, workers gathered at three locations; following immediate dialogue, they were peacefully dispersed within just 15 minutes,” Gautam Buddh Nagar Police Commissioner Laxmi Singh said

title: Logical discrepancy not recognised term under ECI framework, applied only in Bengal: Mamata
content: Addressing a public rally at Pingla in Paschim Medinipur district, she said she has not seen a "dirty party" like the BJP

title: Indian democracy will be stronger, vibrant if women quota implemented in 2029: PM Modi

title: ‘Will not remain silent’: T.N. CM Stalin warns of massive protest against delimitation
content: “Tamil Nadu will come to a standstill. It will register its protest vehemently. Every family will come out to the streets. We will organise a big protest under my leadership,” he said in a video message

title: Elephant strays into Doddabetta Peak; tourist attraction closed
content: Officials said the animal is a sub-adult, which may have recently split from its herd and is trying to find a niche for itself to occupy in the fragmented Nilgiri Biosphere Reserve (NBR) landscape.

title: PM Modi inaugurates Delhi-Dehradun expressway

title: If BJP were playing petty politics, Tamil Nadu would not have got first PM Mitra Park, says Piyush Goyal
content: The first such park was sanctioned in Tamil Nadu’s Virudhunagar, and not in any State ruled by the BJP or NDA, says Union Minister for Commerce and Industry

title: Postpone West Bengal polls until all voters are on rolls, says Adhir Ranjan Chowdhury
content: Five-time Congress MP and former leader of the party in Lok Sabha Adhir Ranjan Chowdhury says incomplete electoral rolls and the Election Commission of India’s SIR process threaten free and fair elections ahead of the West Bengal Assembly elections 2026

title: Mumbai to roll out first non-AC closed-door local train to tackle overcrowding
content: The Integral Coach Factory in Chennai developed the 12-car train featuring compartments, large windows, reasonable leg space, and space to luggage

title: U.P. announces interim hike in minimum wages across categories after Noida protest
content: Unskilled workers in Noida and Ghaziabad will get a 21% increase in their wages; those in municipalities will receive a 15% raise, while workers in other areas will see a 9% increase

title: Search intensifies for man feared fallen into stream in J&K’s Ramban after assault by cow vigilantes
content: A Special Investigation Team has been constituted and four people have been arrested in connection with the case, which triggered widespread protests and tension in the district along the Jammu-Srinagar National Highway

content: Chief Justice Sushrut Arvind Dharmadhikari and Justice G. Arul Murugan slated to hear the case, filed by a voter from Chepauk-Triplicane constituency, on Wednesday

content: The New Helicopter Facility is a state-of-the-art greenfield facility established for the production of Light Utility Helicopter (LUH) and Prachand LCH

content: S. Roshini, the Villivakkam candidate of the Naam Tamilar Katchi (NTK) and the party’s transgender face in the fray, takes her campaign trail through its narrow streets.

title: Tamil Nadu elections: Stalin interacts with voters during morning walk in K.V. Kuppam constituency

title: SC agrees to hear plea to implement disability-sensitive guidelines for welfare insurance schemes
content: The plea has sought a direction that claims under such welfare insurance schemes be decided on a case-by-case basis through reasoned, humane and non-mechanical decision-making processes

title: Hyderabad paediatrician Sivaranjani Santosh quits IAP citing lack of institutional support after receiving legal notice
content: She points out that global paediatric bodies advise caution regarding long-term use of artificial sweeteners in children, citing insufficient evidence on their impact on gut microbiota and potential metabolic risks, including diabetes

title: WNBA draft: Grading Azzi Fudd to Wings and every team's night
content: One team set the curve with an A+. Three got A grades, including Dallas for picking Azzi Fudd. But one got a D.

title: Fudd goes No. 1, reunites with Bueckers in Dallas
content: Azzi Fudd is on her way to Dallas as the No. 1 pick in the WNBA draft with a $500,000 payday waiting for the former UConn star. Minnesota took Olivia Miles of TCU with the No. 2 pick.

title: Valks GM: Agreed to trade before Johnson picked
content: Golden State GM Ohemaa Nyanin told ESPN that the Valkyries and Storm agreed to trade draft picks before the selection of Flau'jae Johnson.

title: UCLA's 5 1st-round picks, 6 overall set records
content: National champion UCLA became the first team to have five first-round picks in the WNBA draft -- Lauren Betts, Gabriela Jaquez, Kiki Rice, Angela Dugalic and Gianna Kneepkens -- and six overall, with Charlisse Leger-Walker taken in the second round.

title: Judge, Trout each homer twice, but Yanks prevail
content: On a night when Los Angeles star Mike Trout hit two home runs against New York, Yankees star Aaron Judge also homered twice in what turned into a heavyweight slugfest, one that the host team won 11-10 at Yankee Stadium in a series opener Monday.

title: ESPN's Vitale announces melanoma diagnosis
content: ESPN Hall of Fame broadcaster Dick Vitale has announced he has melanoma in his lung and liver cavity and will be starting immunotherapy soon.

title: Red Sox ace Crochet hammered for 11 runs, 9 hits
content: Red Sox ace Garrett Crochet was battered by the Twins for 11 runs -- 10 earned -- in 1⅔ innings Monday night.

title: Quick, 40, 3-time Cup champion, plays last game
content: In his final game after nearly two decades in the NHL, goaltender Jonathan Quick, 40, who won the Stanley Cup three times in a decorated career, made 14 saves in the New York Rangers' 3-2 loss to the Florida Panthers on Monday night at Amerant Bank Arena.

title: Ex-Cardinals coach, Titans assistant McGinnis dies
content: Former Tennessee assistant Dave McGinnis, who also served as head coach for the Arizona Cardinals in a decorated career on the NFL sidelines, died at 74 on Monday with his family by his side at Ascension St. Thomas Midtown Hospital, the Titans announced.

title: 🥶 'Drake's seats froze': Raptors lean into buzz
content: The seats underwent a pregame makeover presumably for the rollout of the Canadian rapper's upcoming album, "Iceman."

content: Tottenham are really bad and looking at the way this team has been constructed, one thing clearly stands out: This team is really bad at passing the ball, and that may explain their present malaise.

content: Lisandro Martinez was sent off on Monday for pulling the hair of Leeds United striker Dominic Calvert-Lewin.

title: Fury, Joshua and Wilder are back; what's next for the glamour division?
content: The heavyweight division is buzzing again thanks to Tyson Fury calling out Anthony Joshua and Deontay Wilder's return.

title: Transfer rumors, news: PSG battle Premier League trio for Newcastle youngster
content: Newcastle United midfielder Lewis Miley has received attention ahead of the summer, with Paris Saint-Germain, Manchester United, Arsenal and Chelsea. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Who is Samrat Choudhary set to be new Bihar CM and BJP's first leader to take the top post
content: At 57, Choudhary has steadily risen through Bihar’s political ranks and is regarded as one of the BJP’s strongest organisational figures in the state. He currently holds the Home portfolio and has played a central role in the NDA government, often representing the BJP alongside Nitish Kumar at major official and political events.

title: No handshake! India's Harika breaks silence on Uzbek GM's 'Namaste'
content: Harika Dronavalli clarified viral handshake incident with Nodirbek Yakubboev, saying there was no disrespect. He had pre-informed her he cannot do handshakes due to religious beliefs, and her instinctive resignation gesture caused confusion. She urged social media not to blame him. Similar past incidents exist with Vaishali Rameshbabu. Harika stressed mutual respect and focus on chess, despite controversy overshadowing results.

title: Noida protest: Police flag QR code-based WhatsApp 'syndicate', 7 FIRs filed, over 300 held
content: Following violent clashes over wage demands, Noida saw heavy police presence and route marches as investigators flagged a possible organized WhatsApp syndicate. Over 300 arrests were made, and seven FIRs registered, with the situation now under control after an interim 21% wage hike was announced.

title: Google AI CEO’s message to engineer whose Googler buddy said Google is average at AI adoption
content: Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis fired back at software engineer Steve Yegge after a viral post claimed Google's internal AI adoption was no better than John Deere's. Yegge, citing a conversation with a longtime Google tech director, argued a hiring freeze and reluctance to use Claude Code had left the company stuck. Google's Addy Osmani countered with numbers: 40,000 engineers doing agentic coding weekly.

title: Fan gives Kavya Maran a rose after SRH win - what she did next goes viral
content: Debutant pacers Hinge and Hussain picked four wickets each as Sunrisers Hyderabad ended table-toppers Rajasthan Royals’ winning streak in emphatic fashion. On a surface where the odd ball held up, Kishan struck eight fours and six sixes in a blistering 44-ball 91 to power SRH to 216/6. Anything full and wide was punished through the off side with ease, while short balls were dispatched with authority, helping the skipper bring up his 19th IPL fifty.

title: 'PM Modi is a deshdrohi': Rahul repeats his 'sold India in US trade deal' claim
content: In a two-pronged attack , Rahul Gandhi accused the BJP and the RSS of destroying the Constitution with their "hate-filled thinking", while claiming the ruling TMC has failed in fulfilling its promise of providing jobs to the state's youth. Addressing poll rallies in Raiganj and Malda, Gandhi claimed that 84 lakh youths in Bengal have applied for the unemployment allowance promised by the Mamata Banerjee government.

title: TCS CEO on IT dying by 2030: Companies will need more services for AI
content: TCS CEO K Krithivasan dismisses predictions of India's IT sector becoming obsolete by 2030, asserting that AI will amplify the need for IT services. He highlights the industry's resilience due to deep skill sets, not just cost.

title: Dhurandhar 2 box office collection day 27: Ranveer Singh film inches toward Rs 1100 cr
content: Aditya Dhar’s espionage magnum opus, 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge', continues its unprecedented box office reign on Day 27. Having already made history as the first Hindi film to breach the Rs 1,000 crore India net milestone, the Ranveer Singh-starrer is now closing in on the Rs 1,100 crore domestic landmark. Despite the 2026 IPL season, the film's global footprint has expanded to a staggering Rs 1,718 crore. Stay updated with live tracking, weekly breakdowns, and day-wise earnings of this cinematic phenomenon.

title: 'Sab fake hai': Praful recreates Siraj moment after SRH win - WATCH
content: Debut sensation Praful Hinge etched his name in IPL history by claiming three wickets in the opening over against Rajasthan Royals. His remarkable spell, finishing with 4/34, propelled Sunrisers Hyderabad to a dominant 57-run victory.  Following the win, Hinge recreated a Mohammed Siraj-style video, humorously clarifying his official social media presence.

title: US navy revives 'tanker war' playbook, launches mine-clearing ops in Persian Gulf
content: The US Navy has once again entered the Strait of Hormuz to hunt mines laid by the Iranians. This a repeat of the role played by the US Navy during the Tanker War, a part of the almost 8-year long Iran-Iraq War.

title: Amid FCC dispute between Amazon & SpaceX, Musk compliments Bezos' photograph of Blue Origin rocket
content: Despite ongoing FCC complaints, Jeff Bezos shared a photo of Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket, prompting a rare compliment from Elon Musk. The image signals the rocket's third mission, which will feature a reused booster. Meanwhile, SpaceX is pushing back against Amazon's FCC petition by highlighting Blue Origin's own satellite plans.

title: Explained: What is Trump trying to do with Hormuz blockade & what it means for Iran & US?
content: Ship movements have already dropped sharply, with daily crossings falling to single digits compared with around 135 during normal conditions. A blockade similar to earlier US actions off Venezuela could bring traffic to a complete halt.

title: PM Modi inaugurates Delhi-Dehradun expressway: Check route, toll, travel time
content: Delhi-Dehradun Expressway: Passing through Delhi, Uttar Pradesh and Uttarakhand, the route is expected to bring down the current travel time of over six hours to roughly two and a half hours, according to an official statement.

title: Rockets, drones & missiles: Can Hezbollah sustain a full-scale war with Israel?
content: The Israel-Lebanon border is tense as Hezbollah, despite Israeli efforts, demonstrates operational capability and strategic patience. Its dual power structure with the Lebanese Armed Forces and a sophisticated, adaptable arsenal, including extensive small arms, anti-tank systems, and rocket artillery, presents a significant challenge. This resilience shapes Lebanon's military leverage against Israel, creating a volatile balance.

title: Petrol, diesel losses: What oil firms are losing amid Hormuz supply squeeze
content: India's state-run oil companies are incurring significant losses, estimated at Rs 18 per litre on petrol and Rs 35 on diesel, due to unchanged retail prices amidst volatile global crude oil prices. Despite a government excise duty cut, these firms continue to face mounting financial strain, with potential price hikes anticipated post-state elections.

title: KKR's Blessing Muzarabani banned for two years for picking IPL over PSL
content: PCB banned Muzarabani from the PSL for two years. "In this instance, despite a clear offer and an unequivocal acceptance of essential terms, the player chose to disregard these obligations in favor of a conflicting arrangement," PCB said in a statement, as cited by the Cricbuzz.

title: Bengal bizman found dead with gunshot wound on head, police detain ‘girlfriend’, aide
content: A 37-year-old man, Arup Mondal, was found dead with a gunshot wound in his Kolkata flat. His family alleges foul play, pointing to a financial dispute with his alleged girlfriend, who has been detained. Two firearms were recovered. Investigators are probing whether it was murder or suicide, with family members strongly suggesting a planned killing.

title: JAC 11th Result 2026 out: 99%+ pass, check link
content: The Jharkhand Academic Council, Ranchi announced the Class XI Examination 2026 results after conducting exams across the state. Out of 3,58,533 students who appeared, 3,55,399 were promoted, recording a 99.12 per cent pass rate. Both male and female students showed consistent performance with high success percentages. The results were declared by Chairman Dr. Natwa Hansdak in Ranchi, with officials present, marking the completion of the evaluation process without reported irregularities.

title: UP hikes minimum wages across categories amid Noida protest: What workers will now earn
content: Uttar Pradesh government has approved a significant interim hike of approximately 21% in minimum wages for workers in Gautam Buddh Nagar and Ghaziabad, effective April 1. This decision follows widespread factory worker protests. New monthly wages for unskilled, semi-skilled, and skilled workers in these regions have been revised, with the CM urging employers to ensure fair practices and benefits.

title: How much will Iran lose? The cost of Trump’s Hormuz blockade
content: Following failed talks, the US has imposed a blockade on Iranian ports in the Strait of Hormuz, potentially costing Iran $435 million daily in economic damage, primarily from lost oil and petrochemical exports. This move, aimed at pressuring Iran, could also impact China's energy imports and requires significant US naval commitment to enforce.

title: POSH failure at TCS Nashik? HR AGM arrested for 'ignoring' complaints; emails, chats under scanner
content: A major probe into alleged sexual harassment at a Nashik TCS BPO unit has led to nine FIRs and multiple arrests, including a senior HR official. Eight women employees reported misconduct and inaction by HR. Investigators are scrutinizing digital evidence and internal processes, with TCS assuring strict action and suspending accused employees.

title: Virat Kohli’s ‘very slow knock’ under fire, ex-cricketer questions RCB star
content: Aakash Chopra criticised Virat Kohli’s slow 50 off 38 balls in RCB’s win over Mumbai Indians, calling it “unbecoming” despite the result. He also questioned Kohli’s mindset and noted wasted reviews. Chopra praised Phil Salt’s explosive 78 and Rajat Patidar’s striking form, highlighting their key roles in RCB’s 18-run victory.

title: Trump’s blockade of Strait of Hormuz begins: How will India be impacted?
content: The Strait of Hormuz is a narrow stretch which is important for global trade flows, and is alone responsible for the transit of a fifth of the world’s oil supply. Asian economies like India and China source a big chunk of their crude oil, LPG, and LNG requirements via this strait.

title: 'Muh utha ke nahi maar sakte': Babar triggered by strike-rate question - Watch
content: Babar, however, could not find rhythm and was dismissed for 18 off 20 balls. Babar Azam's slow strike rate in T20s has been a topic of discussion in recent times, and Steve Smith denying him a single in the Big Bash League (BBL) earlier was a topic of discussion in cricketing circles.

title: Puthandu 2026: Best Tamil new year messages, quotes, wishes and images to share
content: Puthandu, the Tamil New Year, is celebrated on April 14th, 2026, marking a time for family gatherings, traditional feasts like mango pachadi, and welcoming prosperity. This ancient festival, rooted in the solar calendar and legends, blends Hindu traditions, agrarian joy, and enduring family bonds, offering a cultural reset for all.

title: ‘Blank mind’ & bold leap: How Harika Dronavalli cracked freestyle chess on debut
content: In a nation where only four women have ever earned the GM title, 35-year-old Harika remains one of its most enduring pillars. As her compatriots prepare for the traditional grind of World Championship cycles, Harika has qualified for the inaugural FIDE Women’s Freestyle Chess World Championship, which is set to take place next year.

title: TCS Nashik row: Undercover op reveals sex abuse, religious pressure; firm vows 'zero tolerance'
content: A tip-off about a Hindu woman observing Ramzan fasts at a TCS Nashik BPO unit triggered a police investigation. Undercover operations led to arrests of seven employees amid allegations of sexual exploitation, religious coercion, and workplace harassment. TCS has suspended employees and launched an internal probe.

title: 'Picture abhi baaki hai': Iran's Bollywood jab at Trump; warns missile boats 'warming up'
content: Iran's consulate in Mumbai pushed back on US President Donald Trump's claims of its navy being "finished." Showcasing fast missile boats and referencing a Shah Rukh Khan movie dialogue, Iran warned the US that "the movie is still left," asserting their defense systems remain operational and control over the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Should Vaibhav Sooryavanshi be picked for Ireland tour?
content: "He is in contention for the Ireland tour and the selectors have shortlisted his name along with those of many other players," a BCCI source was quoted as saying by the Indian Express.

title: Where are gold prices headed amid US-Iran peace talks failure? Check outlook for today
content: Gold price prediction today: Gold prices may trade range-bound with a bearish bias as a breakthrough in US-Iran peace talks remains elusive, says Praveen Singh, Head Currencies and Commodities, Mirae Asset ShareKhan.

title: ‘To be honest…’: Riyan Parag breaks silence after heavy defeat to SRH
content: Riyan Parag termed Rajasthan Royals’ heavy loss to Sunrisers Hyderabad a “blip” after a dramatic top-order collapse. He credited SRH’s debutant pacers for their impact, reflected on tactical decisions, and praised his team’s fightback after being 9/5. Parag remained optimistic, backing RR to recover quickly and perform better in their next match.

title: China exports stumble, imports surge amid Iran war; US shipments down 26.5%
content: China's export growth significantly decelerated to 2.5% in March, falling short of expectations and marking the weakest performance in six months. This slowdown is attributed to rising global energy costs and supply disruptions stemming from the Middle East conflict. Conversely, imports surged by 27.8%, indicating robust domestic demand despite geopolitical uncertainties.

title: Asha Bhosle's granddaughter Zanai Bhosle expresses gratitude for honoring her grandmother

Sharing a funeral image, she expressed that Asha Bhosle lives on in everyone’s hearts and will always be remembered. Read on to know more in detail.

title: 20-yr pause of uranium enrichment & Tehran's counter: How US–Iran talks collapsed at the last mile
content: Peace talks between the US and Iran have stalled over uranium enrichment curbs, with Washington proposing a 20-year freeze and Tehran offering five years. Disagreements also arose regarding the scope of a potential deal, including Hormuz and sanctions relief, despite initial hopes for a breakthrough.
